verses.text: Les trois amis de Job (voir **2.11** ) n'ont pas réussi à le réconforter ( **6.14-30 ** ; **13.4 ** ; **16.2 ** ; **19.21 ** ; **42.10** ).

Bouz avait des relations avec Aram ( **Gn 22.21** ) et l'Arabie ( **Jr 25.23** ), tout comme Ous ( **Jb 1.1** ). • Élihou a déclaré à plusieurs reprises qu'il était en colère ; il considérait probablement sa colère comme un zèle juste ( **Ps 69.9** ).

32:3 Also against his three friends was his wrath kindled,.... He did not take part with either side, but blamed both, and took upon him to be a moderator between them, and deal impartially with them: what highly displeased him, and raised his spirit against the three friends of Job, was, because they had found no answer; they were at a loss for one, for a sufficient one; they had all of them been answering him in their turns again and again, but with nothing to the purpose, not with anything conclusive and convincing;
Le respect d'Élihou envers les anciens était conforme à un principe bien connu (voir aussi **12.12 ** ; **15.10 ** ; **Lv 19.32 ** ; **1Tm 5.1 ** ; **1P 5.5** ).
Le respect d'Élihou envers les anciens était conforme à un principe bien connu (voir aussi **12.12 ** ; **15.10 ** ; **Lv 19.32 ** ; **1Tm 5.1 ** ; **1P 5.5** ).
Le respect d'Élihou envers les anciens était conforme à un principe bien connu (voir aussi **12.12 ** ; **15.10 ** ; **Lv 19.32 ** ; **1Tm 5.1 ** ; **1P 5.5** ).
Le respect d'Élihou envers les anciens était conforme à un principe bien connu (voir aussi **12.12 ** ; **15.10 ** ; **Lv 19.32 ** ; **1Tm 5.1 ** ; **1P 5.5** ).

L'esprit présent à l'intérieur des gens n'est peut-être pas l'Esprit prophétique dont parle Élihou plus tard ( **33.14-15** ), comme l'avait fait Élifaz ( **4.12-17 ** ; cf. **32.18-20** ). Cet esprit est peut-être inhérent à la création ( **33.4 ** ; **Gn 2.7 ** ; **Ac 17.25** ).

L'esprit présent à l'intérieur des gens n'est peut-être pas l'Esprit prophétique dont parle Élihou plus tard ( **33.14-15** ), comme l'avait fait Élifaz ( **4.12-17 ** ; cf. **32.18-20** ). Cet esprit est peut-être inhérent à la création ( **33.4 ** ; **Gn 2.7 ** ; **Ac 17.25** ).

32:10 Therefore I said, hearken to me,.... Thou O Job, and every one of you his friends, for the word is singular; though I am but a young man, yet if I have the Spirit of God, and am under the inspiration of the Almighty, I may be able to say something to you worth your hearing: I also will show mine opinion; for though for a while he was timorous and fearful of doing it, lest he should mistake and expose himself, yet having duly weighed and considered the above things, he was determined to do it.

32:11 I waited for your words; I gave ear to your reasons - Instead of תבונותיכם tebunotheychem, your reasons, תכונותיכם techunotheychem, your arguments, is the reading of nine of Kennicott's and De Rossi's MSS. The sense, however, is nearly the same. Whilst ye searched out what to say - עד תחקרון מלין ad tachkerun millin; "Whilst ye were searching up and down for words." A fine irony, which they must have felt.

32:12 Yea, I attended unto you - Instead of ועדיכם veadeychem, and unto you, one MS. reads the above letters with points that cause it to signify and your testimonies; which is the reading of the Syriac, Arabic, and Septuagint. Behold, there was none of you that convinced Job - Confuted Job. They spoke multitudes of words, but were unable to overthrow his arguments.

32:13 This has been so ordered, "lest you should" pride yourselves on having overcome him by your "wisdom" (Jer 9:23, the great aim of the Book of Job); and that you may see, "God alone can thrust him down," that is, confute him, "not man." So Elihu grounds his confutation, not on the maxims of sages, as the friends did, but on his special commission from God (Job 32:8; Job 33:4, Job 33:6).

32:14 He hath not directed - I am no party in this controversy; I have no party feeling in it: he has not spoken a word against me, therefore I have no cause of irritation. I shall speak for truth; not for conquest or revenge. Neither will I answer him with your speeches; your passions have been inflamed by contradiction, and you have spoken foolishly with your lips.

Ici, il y a deux possibilités de traduction : soit les paroles d'Élihou sont adressées aux autres amis de Job (ex. "Vous êtes abattus..."), soit Élihou s'adresse à Job en parlant de ses amis (ex. "Ils sont abattus...").

Ici, il y a deux possibilités de traduction : soit les paroles d'Élihou sont adressées aux autres amis de Job (ex. "Vous êtes abattus..."), soit Élihou s'adresse à Job en parlant de ses amis (ex. "Ils sont abattus...").

32:17 I will answer also my part - אענה חלקי aaneh chelki, "I will recite my portion." We have already seen that the book of Job is a sort of drama, in which several persons have their different parts to recite. Probably the book was used in this way, in ancient times, for the sake of public instruction. Eliphaz, Zophar, and Bildad, had recited their parts, and Job had responded to each: nothing was brought to issue. Elihu, a bystander, perceiving this, comes forward and takes a part, when all the rest had expended their materials: yet Elihu, though he spoke well, was incapable of closing the controversy;

La longueur du discours d'Élihou ( **32.1-37.24** ) montre qu'il était vraiment plein de paroles refoulées. • Élihou pensait probablement que l'esprit (en hébreu ruakh ) en lui était une urgence prophétique ( **32.8** , **19-20 ** ; **Ps 39.3** ), mais le lecteur pourrait le trouver plein de "vent " (hébreu ruakh ; voir la note d'étude sur Job 15.2-3 ). • Comme Sofar, Élihou doit parler pour trouver un soulagement (cf. **20.2** ).

La longueur du discours d'Élihou ( **32.1-37.24** ) montre qu'il était vraiment plein de paroles refoulées. • Élihou pensait probablement que l'esprit (en hébreu ruakh ) en lui était une urgence prophétique ( **32.8** , **19-20 ** ; **Ps 39.3** ), mais le lecteur pourrait le trouver plein de "vent " (hébreu ruakh ; voir la note d'étude sur Job 15.2-3 ). • Comme Sofar, Élihou doit parler pour trouver un soulagement (cf. **20.2** ).

32:21 "May I never accept," &c. Elihu alludes to Job's words (Job 13:8, Job 13:10), wherein he complains that the friends plead for God partially, "accepting His person." Elihu says he will not do so, but will act impartially between God and Job. "And I will not give flattery," &c. (Pro 24:23).

32:22 My Maker would soon take me away - Were I to copy this conduct while under the influence which I now feel, God might justly consume me as in a moment. He is my Maker; he made me to know truth, to tell truth, and to live according to truth; for he is the God of truth: I shall, therefore through his help, speak the Truth, the Whole Truth, and Nothing but the Truth. We find from the above that vain titles of ceremony, expressive of the most eminent qualities, were given to worthless men, from time immemorial;
